impact: `<button class="b-hamburger" onclick="document.getElementById('mobile-menu').classList.toggle('is-open')" aria-label="Toggle menu">` (base.html:876) lacks `aria-expanded` (screen readers can't announce open/closed state), `aria-controls` (no relationship to the menu region), and no ESC key handler. The menu is `position:fixed; top:46px; bottom:0` — when open, background body remains scrollable so users can scroll the page content behind the menu. The hamburger is the only way to close it (no overlay click-to-close, no X button at top). Recommended fix: convert to a small inline JS toggle that flips `aria-expanded` on the button, adds `aria-controls="mobile-menu"`, locks `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'` when open, and registers an `Escape` keyup listener. Mobile menu structure itself is fine — fixes are all on the trigger and lifecycle.
resolution: Fixed in commit 12641e2. Trigger button now has data-click="toggleMobileMenu" (T-031 delegation), aria-expanded mirrors menu state, aria-controls="mobile-menu", and aria-hidden flips on the menu region. window.toggleMobileMenu uses a setOpen() helper that synchronizes all three a11y attrs and toggles body.style.overflow for scroll lock. ESC keydown listener dismisses the menu when open. Verified post-deploy: healthz 200, commit 12641e2.
title: Mobile menu missing aria-expanded, body-scroll lock, and ESC dismiss
